subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF Art IV, S&P, Freedom House, RSF, EEA/EFTA, Council of Europe) + international/European press.
A wealthy constitutional monarchy with AAA-equivalent fiscal strength (near-zero public debt) - uniquely an EEA member while Switzerland is not, in a Swiss-franc customs/monetary union.

Standout features: exceptional balance sheet, EEA + Swiss market access, a safe-haven CHF, top-tier safety and press freedom, against an unusually powerful monarch (a democratic-quality caveat) and SNB-imported monetary policy.

economic.notes: An exceptional balance sheet and diversified high-value base; CHF-anchored, no monetary autonomy.

fiscal_state.notes: AAA (S&P), virtually zero public debt, and financial assets ~100% of GDP - exceptional fiscal strength. (IMF Art IV 2025; S&P)

monetary_independence.notes: A Swiss-franc customs/monetary union - imports SNB policy, no own monetary policy. (IMF; SNB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Uses the Swiss franc (a premier safe-haven currency) but issues none of it. (IMF; SNB)

inflation.notes: Very low, expected below ~1% over the medium term (the CHF anchor). (IMF Art IV 2025)

banking_stability.notes: A sound, well-capitalized financial center (~20% of GDP), private-banking/wealth-focused with robust supervision. (IMF Art IV 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: High-value manufacturing (Hilti etc.) plus financial services, with EEA single-market access and a Swiss union - strong and diversified for its size. (IMF; EEA)

institutional.notes: Strong rule of law, credible stats, and a free press, with a princely-power democratic caveat.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong rule of law - FH 90/100, human rights generally respected. (Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible official statistics, IMF-engaged and EEA-aligned. (IMF Art IV)

civil_service_capacity.notes: An efficient, well-resourced administration managing EEA obligations. (IMF; EEA)

press_freedom.notes: RSF #7 globally (2025) - among the world's freest and independent, with a tiny media market. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Very stable and prosperous, with the Prince's unusually strong powers a democratic caveat.

political_stability.notes: Very stable - the Feb 2025 election produced a renewed VU-FBP coalition under the first female PM (April 2025); the Prince's veto (incl. over plebiscites) and dismissal powers, confirmed by the contested 2003 referendum, are a democratic-quality caveat. (2025 results; Council of Europe)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A large foreign-resident/cross-border workforce generally well treated, with restrictive naturalization. (FH 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: A cohesive, prosperous society with mild immigration/cross-border tensions. (FH)

demographics.notes: Small (~40k) and aging, with cross-border commuters sustaining the labour force. (IMF)

geopolitical.notes: EEA + Swiss-anchored, conflict-free, with a safe-haven currency setting.

alliance_reliability.notes: Not in NATO/EU, but uniquely an EEA member plus a Swiss customs/monetary union (a UN/Council of Europe/EFTA member). (EEA; EFTA)

conflict_involvement.notes: No conflict exposure and no standing army. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account, EEA/Swiss-aligned, with a reformed AML/transparency regime and a safe-haven CHF setting. (IMF; EEA)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent Swiss-integrated services and very low crime, with low hazard exposure.

climate.notes: An Alpine micro-state - very high adaptive capacity but glacier/snow and Rhine-flood exposure. (IPCC AR6 mountains; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Excellent and well-funded, integrated with Swiss/Austrian systems for complex care. (general)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime - among the safest. (general; FH)

disaster_insurance.notes: A developed Swiss-integrated insurance market with low catastrophe exposure. (general)

summary: A wealthy, very stable constitutional monarchy with AAA-equivalent fiscal strength - virtually zero public debt and financial assets near 100% of GDP - anchored to the Swiss franc and uniquely holding EEA single-market membership while Switzerland does not. Its economy combines a sound ~20%-of-GDP financial center with world-class high-value manufacturing (Hilti and others), and it scores top-tier on safety, press freedom (RSF #7), and institutions (FH 90/100). The one genuine caveat is democratic: the Prince retains unusually strong powers - veto over legislation and even popular votes, plus the right to dismiss the government, confirmed by the contested 2003 referendum - though day-to-day politics are calm (a renewed coalition under the country's first female PM took office in April 2025). It imports SNB monetary policy but benefits from the safe-haven CHF; overall clearly positive skew.
